fan_summary_en: Norway can connect Odegaard's passing, Nusa's carrying, and Haaland or Sorloth finishing in the same move, but the space behind the fullbacks can make matches swing fast.

tactical_features.in_possession_shape: Base back four with frequent 2-3-5 or 2-4-4 attacking occupation; Odegaard advances into the right half-space and Nusa drives the left side.
tactical_features.out_of_possession_shape: Usually 4-4-2 or 4-4-1-1 with Odegaard joining the first line.
alternate_shapes: Sorloth starting wide often becomes a narrow second-forward profile
Without Odegaard the attack becomes more direct and less precise between the lines
build_up_style: find Odegaard in the right half-space
use Nusa as the main left-side carrier
play early into Haaland or Sorloth when space opens
preferred_progression_zones: early direct ball behind the line
pressing_triggers: wide receiver with back to goal
tactical_features.rest_defense_shape: Often 2+1 or 2+2 when fullbacks push high.

leading_behavior: stabilize midfield or back line while keeping one transition outlet
prefer functional tweaks over full structural reset
drawing_behavior: keep the main structure and continue to lean on Odegaard and Nusa chance creation
trailing_behavior: likely to add wing dribbling or a second aerial option
strong evidence for a fixed chase script is still limited

attacking_ceiling.note: Odegaard, Nusa, Haaland, and Sorloth give Norway top-end chance creation and finishing variety.

defensive_floor.note: Aggressive fullback positioning can expose recovery defense and lower the floor.

set_piece_upside.note: Delivery plus multiple aerial targets gives Norway strong set-piece upside.

tempo_profile.note: Norway can control field position against weaker teams and accelerate quickly in transition.

totals_tendency.note: Matches can become open when Nusa is carrying, fullbacks push high, and Norway attack with multiple box targets.

player_dependency_risk.note: Chance creation depends heavily on Odegaard and Nusa; elite finishing depends on Haaland or Sorloth.

market_relevant_tactical_flags.direction_note: Without him, Norway loses precision against organized blocks and on set pieces.
With him fully fit, Norway is more likely to create open-field and one-v-one attacks.

market_relevant_tactical_flags.factor: Sorloth role as central striker or narrow wide starter
market_relevant_tactical_flags.direction_note: His role changes cross volume, second-ball pressure, and two-target box presence.
A more controlling pivot tends to raise defensive stability; a more physical profile raises duel and transition volume.
The higher the fullbacks, the higher both Norway's attacking ceiling and counterattack exposure.

role_dependencies.impact_if_missing: Final-ball quality and set-piece delivery drop.
The team loses its most explosive self-created progression threat.
Penalty-box occupation, finishing, and aerial threat fall.
Right-side dribbling and inside-creation variety drop, but the whole attack does not collapse.
